flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is an equivalence relation if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. It partitions the set into disjoint equivalence classes.
A relation R on set A is reflexive if (a,a) ∈ R for every element a ∈ A. Every element relates to itself.
A relation R on set A is symmetric if whenever (a,b) ∈ R, then (b,a) ∈ R for all a,b ∈ A.
A relation R on set A is transitive if whenever (a,b) ∈ R and (b,c) ∈ R, then (a,c) ∈ R for all a,b,c ∈ A.
For an element a ∈ A, the equivalence class [a] = {x ∈ A | xRa} is the set of all elements related to a by equivalence relation R.

flashcards.front: Define a partition of a set
flashcards.back: A partition of set A is a collection of non-empty, pairwise disjoint subsets whose union equals A. Each element belongs to exactly one block.
The quotient set A/R is the set of all equivalence classes of A under relation R. A/R = {[a] | a ∈ A}.
For integers a and b, a ≡ b (mod n) if n divides (a-b). This is an equivalence relation on Z with n equivalence classes.
An element chosen from an equivalence class to represent the entire class. Often the smallest nonnegative integer in the class.

flashcards.front: State the Fundamental Theorem of Equivalence Relations
flashcards.back: There is a bijective correspondence between equivalence relations on A and partitions of A. Each equivalence relation determines a partition and vice versa.

flashcards.front: What are congruence classes modulo n?
flashcards.back: The congruence class [a]n = {a + kn | k ∈ Z}. There are exactly n distinct classes: [0], [1], ..., [n-1].

flashcards.front: When are two equivalence classes equal?
flashcards.back: [a] = [b] if and only if aRb (i.e., a is related to b by the equivalence relation). Otherwise, [a] ∩ [b] = ∅.

flashcards.front: What is the union of equivalence classes equal to?
flashcards.back: The union of all equivalence classes equals the original set A. ⋃{[a] | a ∈ A} = A.
A relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. Examples: ≤ on real numbers, subset relation ⊆ on power sets.
An equivalence relation used in computer science where elements are considered equivalent if they have compatible attributes or satisfy certain conditions.
